insight:
Yesterday was a mixed bag with just one day to go! Your body showed some great signs - that resting heart rate of 50 bpm is fantastic and your stress level at 24/100 is impressively low. You burned 2,326 calories which hit your daily target perfectly, but those 5,559 steps suggest you kept things very light, which is actually smart pre-race strategy.

However, that 4.7 hours of sleep is concerning this close to race day. Your HRV at 95ms sits right at your baseline lower end, which combined with the poor sleep suggests your body is working hard to recover. With the half marathon tomorrow, stick to your planned easy activities today - no changes needed, but prioritize rest completely.

Focus intensely on sleep tonight. Given yesterday's short sleep hurt your recovery, aim for 8+ hours tonight. Skip any caffeine after 2pm today and consider a warm bath before bed to help your body fully prepare for tomorrow's big day.
